feedback.text: From a Stop Killing Games / Stop Destroying Videogames supporter (me): I want to politely ask the people behind the Digital Fairness Act to consider (consumer) protections against the practices from videogame companies that are destroying videogames. An increasing amount of videogame companies are starting to require paid videogames (and content) to be connected to the internet / servers in order to play them. The big problem is; when the companies are shutting down the servers forever, then all working copies of these games (and content) will be destroyed forever. Besides of that, the companies are not willing to come with "End-of-Life plans" for these games. End-of-Life plans to keep these games playable in some form. This is a very hostile move towards paying consumers and those that want to own-, to collect-, or to preserve videogames. Without any (consumer) protections against these practices, the problematic practices from the gaming industry will continue and things will get worse. In the near future, the amount of videogames with an online requirement (that will not get an End-of-Life plan when the servers will shutdown) will drastically increase. There will also be an increase of games with a Hybrid-Cloud requirement (like Call of Duty: Black Ops 6, Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, Pax Dei, etc.), and, Cloud Streaming exclusive games (like the completely destroyed game Outcasters) (that will not get an End-of-Life plan when the servers will shutdown). In the further future, things will get far worse because publicly traded companies want streaming (from the cloud) to replace all forms of traditional gaming. They want a streaming-only future (with full power and control over everyone and everything) where ownership, preservation, videogames sold as a product, collecting videogames, old(er) videogames, gaming history, traditional gaming hardware, local / offline computing, videogame retailers, digital stores (as we know them), computer stores (as we know them), several communities, etc. cannot exist anymore.
